## `library.cardgame` {#cardgame}

*171 functions documented, 8 classes*

```lua
library.cardgame.getIdCounter(  ) -> number  -- Return the current value of the internal ID counter (next ID to assign). Lua doubles are exact up to 2^53 (9007199254740992).
library.cardgame.resetIdCounter(  )  -- Reset the ID counter to 1.  Call between game sessions to reclaim the integer range.  Does NOT invalidate already-created cards � callers must ensure no stale references remain.
library.cardgame.defineCardType( name : string, def : table )  -- Register or overwrite a card type definition.
library.cardgame.getCardType( name : string ) -> table|nil  -- Look up a card type by name; returns nil if not found.
library.cardgame.getCardTypeNames(  ) -> table  -- Return a sorted list of all registered type names.
library.cardgame.clearCardTypes(  )  -- Clear all card type definitions from the module registry.
library.cardgame.newCardTypeDef( name : string ) -> table  -- Create a new card type definition (blueprint). CardTypeDef fields:
library.cardgame.newCard( card_type : string ) -> LCard  -- Create a new card instance.  Seeds fields from the registry if the type is defined.  Each card receives a unique auto-incrementing integer ID. Card fields:
library.cardgame.newStack( name : string ) -> LCardStack  -- Create a new unbounded Stack. Stack fields:
library.cardgame.newStackWithCapacity( name : string, cap : number ) -> LCardStack  -- Create a new Stack with a fixed capacity limit.
library.cardgame.newSlot( name : string ) -> Slot  -- Create a new unbounded Slot. Slot fields:
library.cardgame.newSlotWithCapacity( name : string, cap : number ) -> Slot  -- Create a new Slot with a fixed capacity limit.
library.cardgame.newCardPool( name : string ) -> CardPool  -- Create a new empty CardPool. CardPool fields:
library.cardgame.newStackManager(  ) -> StackManager  -- Create a new empty StackManager.
library.cardgame.newBuildEntry( type_name : string, count : number ) -> table  -- Create a new build entry for use with DeckBuilder.
library.cardgame.newDeckBuilder( name : string ) -> DeckBuilder  -- Create a new DeckBuilder.
library.cardgame.HistoryAction.pushed( card_type : string, item_name : string ) -> table  -- Create a Pushed action recording which card was pushed.
library.cardgame.HistoryAction.popped( card_type : string, item_name : string ) -> table  -- Create a Popped action recording which card was popped.
library.cardgame.HistoryAction.moved( card_type : string, item_name : string, from_stack : string, to_stack : string ) -> table  -- Create a Moved action recording an inter-stack card transfer.
library.cardgame.HistoryAction.shuffled(  ) -> table  -- Create a Shuffled action.
library.cardgame.HistoryAction.sorted( by : string ) -> table  -- Create a Sorted action recording the sort field.
library.cardgame.HistoryAction.cleared(  ) -> table  -- Create a Cleared action.
library.cardgame.HistoryAction.built( count : number ) -> table  -- Create a Built action recording how many cards were built.
library.cardgame.HistoryAction.custom( label : string ) -> table  -- Create a user-defined Custom action.
library.cardgame.newStackHistory(  ) -> StackHistory  -- Create a new unlimited StackHistory.
library.cardgame.newStackHistoryWithMaxSize( max_size : number ) -> StackHistory  -- Create a StackHistory that keeps only the most recent max_size entries.
library.cardgame.newCardGroup( label : string, indices : table, score : number|nil ) -> CardGroup  -- Create a new CardGroup with a label, index list, and optional score.
library.cardgame.checkDeckLimit( cards : table ) -> string|nil  -- Validate that a card list does not exceed per-type max_per_deck limits. Returns nil on success or an error string describing the first violation.
library.cardgame.groupByCategory( items : table ) -> table  -- Group card-like items by category field. Returns table: category -> {item,...}.
library.cardgame.findAtLeastNOfStat( items : table, stat : string, n : number ) -> table  -- Return items where getStat(stat) >= n.
library.cardgame.findTagGroups( items : table ) -> table  -- Return each unique tag that appears on more than one item, with the items list.
library.cardgame.sortedIndicesByStat( items : table, stat : string ) -> table  -- Return 1-based indices sorted by a stat (ascending).
library.cardgame.sortedIndicesByCategory( items : table ) -> table  -- Return 1-based indices sorted by category (alphabetical).
library.cardgame.groupByStat( items : table, stat : string ) -> table  -- Group 1-based indices of items by the integer value of a named stat. Returns a map of stat_value (floored to integer) ��� array of 1-based indices.
library.cardgame.groupByTagPrefix( items : table, prefix : string ) -> table  -- Group 1-based indices by the value portion of a prefixed tag. Tags of the form "prefix:value" are grouped under "value". Tags that do not start with prefix: are ignored.
library.cardgame.findNOfStat( items : table, stat : string, n : number ) -> table  -- Find all groups where exactly n items share the same integer stat value. Analogous to "n-of-a-kind" detection in card games.
library.cardgame.findSequences( items : table, stat : string, min_run : number ) -> table  -- Find all runs of consecutive integer stat values with length >= min_run. Useful for "straight" or sequential-run detection in card games. Each run is returned as a CardGroup whose indices reference the original list.
```

```lua
Card:getStat( key : string ) -> number  -- Return the numeric stat value for key, or 0 if not set.

Card:tap(  )  -- Tap this card (mark as used/exhausted).

Card:isTapped(  ) -> boolean  -- Return true if the card is tapped.
```

### `Stack`

```lua
Stack:size(  ) -> number  -- Return the number of cards.
Stack:isEmpty(  ) -> boolean  -- Return true when the stack contains no cards.
Stack:isFull(  ) -> boolean  -- Return true when the stack has reached its capacity limit.
Stack:capacity(  ) -> number|nil  -- Return the capacity limit, or nil for unlimited.
Stack:setCapacity( cap : number|nil )  -- Set or remove the capacity limit (nil = unlimited).
Stack:pushTop( card : LCard ) -> boolean  -- Push a card onto the top of the stack; returns false when full.
Stack:pushBottom( card : LCard ) -> boolean  -- Push a card onto the bottom of the stack; returns false when full.
Stack:popTop(  ) -> LCard|nil  -- Remove and return the top card, or nil if empty.
Stack:popBottom(  ) -> LCard|nil  -- Remove and return the bottom card, or nil if empty.
Stack:popMany( n : number ) -> table  -- Pop up to n cards from the top and return them.
Stack:peekTop(  ) -> LCard|nil  -- Return the top card without removing it.
Stack:peekBottom(  ) -> LCard|nil  -- Return the bottom card without removing it.
Stack:peekAt( idx : number ) -> LCard|nil  -- Return the card at the given 1-based index without removing it.
Stack:insertAt( idx : number, card : LCard ) -> boolean  -- Insert card at position idx (1-based, clamped); returns false when full.
Stack:removeAt( idx : number ) -> LCard|nil  -- Remove and return the card at 1-based position idx, or nil if out of range.
Stack:moveWithin( from : number, to : number ) -> boolean  -- Move a card from one 1-based index to another within the same stack.
Stack:clear(  ) -> table  -- Clear all cards and return them.
Stack:searchByType( card_type : string ) -> table  -- Return a list of 1-based indices of cards with the given type name.
Stack:searchByTag( tag : string ) -> table  -- Return a list of 1-based indices of cards that have the given tag.
Stack:searchByCategory( cat : string ) -> table  -- Return a list of 1-based indices of cards with the given category.
Stack:findByType( card_type : string ) -> number|nil  -- Return the 1-based index of the first card with the given type, or nil.
Stack:findByTag( tag : string ) -> number|nil  -- Return the 1-based index of the first card with the given tag, or nil.
Stack:findByCategoryAll( cat : string ) -> table  -- Return all Card objects with the given category.
Stack:findByTypeAll( type_name : string ) -> table  -- Return all Card objects with the given type name.
Stack:findByTagAll( tag : string ) -> table  -- Return all Card objects that have the given tag.
Stack:removeById( id : number ) -> LCard|nil  -- Remove and return the Card with the given id, or nil if not found.
Stack:containsId( id : number ) -> boolean  -- Return true if a card with the given id is in the stack.
Stack:countByType( t : string ) -> number  -- Return the count of cards with the given type name.
Stack:countByCategory( cat : string ) -> number  -- Return the count of cards with the given category.
Stack:countByTag( tag : string ) -> number  -- Return the count of cards that have the given tag.
Stack:sortByStat( stat : string )  -- Sort cards by a named stat in ascending order (in-place).
Stack:sortByStatDesc( stat : string )  -- Sort cards by a named stat in descending order (in-place).
Stack:sortByCategory(  )  -- Sort cards alphabetically by category field (in-place).
Stack:sortByName(  )  -- Sort cards alphabetically by name field (in-place).
Stack:shuffle(  )  -- Shuffle cards into a random order using Fisher-Yates. TODO(P4 lift): replace with lurek.math.shuffle when available so the shuffle becomes seedable and decoupled from the global RNG state.
Stack:items(  ) -> table  -- Return the raw card array (by reference).
Stack:peekTopNTypes( n : number ) -> table  -- Return the type names of the top n cards (topmost first).
Stack:snapshotCards(  ) -> table  -- Return a shallow copy of the card array for later restoration.
Stack:restoreCards( cards : table )  -- Replace the card array with a previously snapshotted copy.
Stack:isOrdered(  ) -> boolean  -- Return true when the stack preserves insertion order.
Stack:setOrdered( b : boolean )  -- Set whether the stack preserves order.
Stack:isPublic(  ) -> boolean  -- Return true when the stack contents are publicly visible.
Stack:setPublic( b : boolean )  -- Set whether the stack is publicly visible.
Stack:setName( n : string )  -- Rename the stack.
```

### `CardPool`

```lua
CardPool:add( type_name : string, weight : number )  -- Add a type with a draw weight (minimum 1).

CardPool:setWeight( type_name : string, weight : number )  -- Update the weight for an existing type entry (no-op if not found).
CardPool:setName( n : string )  -- Rename the pool.
CardPool:setRarityWeight( rarity : string, weight : number )  -- Set a per-rarity draw weight used by drawByRarity.
CardPool:totalWeight(  ) -> number  -- Return the sum of all entry weights.
CardPool:size(  ) -> number  -- Return the number of distinct entries in the pool.
CardPool:isEmpty(  ) -> boolean  -- Return true when the pool has no entries.
CardPool:getWeight( type_name : string ) -> number  -- Return the draw weight for a type name, or 0 if not present.
CardPool:getTypeNames(  ) -> table  -- Return an array of all type name strings in pool insertion order.
CardPool:drawTypes( n : number ) -> table  -- Draw n type names by weighted random selection (with replacement).
CardPool:drawItems( n : number ) -> table  -- Draw n Card instances by weighted random selection (with replacement).
CardPool:drawUniqueTypes( n : number ) -> table  -- Draw up to n unique type names by weighted selection without replacement.
CardPool:drawUniqueItems( n : number ) -> table  -- Draw up to n unique Card instances by weighted selection without replacement.
CardPool:drawItemsSeeded( n : number, seed : number ) -> table  -- Draw n Card instances using a fixed random seed for reproducibility. Saves and restores the global RNG state across the call so callers outside the seeded scope continue to observe the global RNG sequence. TODO(P4 lift): use lurek.math.newRng()/lurek.math.shuffle when available to avoid touching the global RNG entirely.
CardPool:drawByRarity( distribution : table ) -> table  -- Draw cards matching a rarity distribution table {rarity=count,...}.
```

## `library.cinematic` *(partial)* {#cinematic}

> Lurek2D cinematic library — multi-track scrubbable cutscene timeline.

Sequences `lurek.tween`, `lurek.camera`, `lurek.audio`, `lurek.event`,
and `library.dialog` clips into a single time-positioned timeline that
supports play/pause/seek/scrub/skip-to-label/branch.

Each track is a sorted list of clips: `{at, duration, kind, params,
on_apply, on_revert}`. The timeline owns its own clock — it does not use
`lurek.timer.Scheduler`.

*38 functions documented, 2 classes*

```lua
library.cinematic.newTimeline( opts : table? ) -> Timeline  -- Create a new timeline.
library.cinematic.fromToml( path )  -- Load a timeline from a TOML file via `lurek.filesystem.read` + `lurek.serial.fromToml`.
library.cinematic.fromTable( spec )  -- Build a timeline from a declarative spec table.
```

### `Track`

```lua
Track:add( clip : table )  -- Add a generic clip table.
Track:tween( at, duration, target, props, easing )  -- Tween clip. Wraps `lurek.tween` if available; always applies final value if the engine binding is missing (so logic-only tests still pass).
Track:cameraTo( at, duration, x, y, zoom, easing )  -- Camera move clip.
Track:shake( at, duration, intensity )  -- Camera shake clip.
Track:dialog( at, line )  -- Dialog clip — fires once forward only.
Track:audio( at, source, opts )  -- Audio clip — fires once forward only.
Track:signal( at, name, ... )  -- Signal clip — emits via `lurek.event.push` (or queues for later read).
Track:call( at, fn, opts )  -- Generic Lua callback. Mark `reversible = true` to allow backward seeks.
Track:wait( at, predicate_fn )  -- Wait clip — pauses the timeline until `predicate_fn()` returns true.
Track:remove( clip )  -- Remove a clip by reference.
```

### `Timeline`

```lua
Timeline:track( name )  -- Get-or-create a track by name.
Timeline:tracks(  ) -> table  -- Return an ordered array of all tracks in this timeline.
Timeline:_recompute_duration(  )  -- Recompute total duration from the latest clip end.
Timeline:setDialogHandler( fn )  -- Bind a dialog handler `fn(line)` invoked by `track:dialog` clips.
Timeline:play(  ) -> Timeline  -- Start (or resume) playback of this timeline.
Timeline:pause(  ) -> Timeline  -- Pause playback at the current time position.
Timeline:resume(  ) -> Timeline  -- Resume playback from the current time position.
Timeline:stop(  ) -> Timeline  -- Stop playback and reset the timeline to time zero.
Timeline:isPlaying(  ) -> boolean  -- Return true if the timeline is currently playing.
Timeline:isFinished(  ) -> boolean  -- Return true if the timeline has reached its end and is not looping.
Timeline:getTime(  ) -> number  -- Return the current playback time in seconds.
Timeline:getDuration(  ) -> number  -- Return the total duration of the timeline in seconds.
Timeline:setTimeScale( s : number ) -> Timeline  -- Set the playback speed multiplier (1.0 = real-time, 2.0 = double speed).
Timeline:label( at, name )  -- Add a labelled cue point.
Timeline:branch( at, predicate, child )  -- Add a branch — `child_timeline` runs at `at` only when `predicate(tl)` is true.
Timeline:update( dt )  -- Advance timeline by `dt`. Call once per frame from `lurek.process`.
Timeline:_reset_clip_flags(  )  -- Reset fired/applied flags on all clips in all tracks (used after loop restart).
Timeline:setTime( t )  -- Seek to absolute time.
Timeline:scrub( delta : number ) -> Timeline  -- Seek forward or backward by `delta` seconds relative to current time.
Timeline:rewind(  ) -> Timeline  -- Rewind the timeline to time zero.
Timeline:skipTo( label : string ) -> Timeline  -- Seek to the time position of a named label.
Timeline:onComplete( fn : function ) -> table  -- Register a callback invoked when the timeline finishes. Returns a handle that can be passed to `Timeline:offHandle`.
Timeline:onTrackEnter( name : string, fn : function ) -> table  -- Register a callback invoked whenever a clip on `name` track begins.
Timeline:offHandle( handle : table )  -- Deregister a callback handle previously returned by `onComplete` or `onTrackEnter`.
Timeline:export(  ) -> table  -- Export a lightweight snapshot of the timeline state for serialisation.
```

## `library.combat` {#combat}

*97 functions documented, 7 classes*

```lua
library.combat.newCollisionGroupSet(  ) -> CollisionGroupSet New instance with no groups or rules.  -- Creates an empty collision-group set.
library.combat.newMountSlot( id : string, x : number, y : number, size_class : string ) -> table  -- Creates a new turret or weapon mount slot.
library.combat.newChassis( body_id : number, max_hp : number ) -> Chassis  -- Creates a new chassis with the given physics body ID and maximum hit points.
library.combat.newTurret( body_id : number, joint_id : number ) -> Turret  -- Creates a new turret with the given physics body and joint IDs.
library.combat.newWeapon( name : string ) -> Weapon  -- Creates a new weapon with default values. Defaults: fire_rate=1, ammo=-1 (infinite), damage_amount=10, range=500, projectile_speed=300, burst_size=1.
library.combat.newProjectilePool( pool_size : number, projectile_type : string ) -> ProjectilePool  -- Creates a new projectile pool with the given capacity. Defaults to DEFAULT_POOL_SIZE (64) when pool_size is nil; capped at MAX_POOL_SIZE (1024).
library.combat.newCombatWorld(  ) -> CombatWorld New world with no entities.  -- Creates an empty combat world. This is a logical container only — broad-phase hit detection and shape queries should be performed against a real physics world via `lurek.physics.newWorld():raycast()` / `:shapecast()` and the resulting contacts then mapped back onto chassis/turret/weapon entities here.
```

## `library.dialog` {#dialog}

> A pure-Lua replacement for the former `lurek.dialog` Rust binding.
No engine dependencies; works in headless test VMs.
Optional: uses `lurek.log.debug()` when available for dialog progression tracing.

Usage:
local dialog = require("library.dialog")
local seq = dialog.newSequencer()
seq:setSpeed(25)
seq:on("line", function(speaker, text) print(speaker..": "..text) end)
seq:load({ {type="say",speaker="Alice",text="Hello!"} })
seq:start()
-- each frame: seq:update(dt)
-- on input:   seq:advance()  /  seq:choose(1)

*26 functions documented, 1 classes*

```lua
library.dialog.newSequencer(  ) -> table  -- Create a new dialog sequencer. The sequencer runs a list of dialog nodes one at a time, revealing typewriter-style text, pausing for choices, and firing named callbacks. States: "idle"    ÔÇö no script loaded or sequence ended, not started "typing"  ÔÇö revealing the current line character by character "waiting" ÔÇö current line fully revealed, waiting for advance() "choice"  ÔÇö waiting for the player to call choose(index) "paused"  ÔÇö a "wait" node is counting down "done"    ÔÇö sequence finished
library.dialog.say( actor : string, text : string, opts : table ) -> table  -- Create a `say` dialog node (spoken line with typewriter reveal).
library.dialog.choice( prompt : string, options : table, opts : table ) -> table  -- Create a `choice` dialog node (branching prompt).
library.dialog.wait( seconds : number, opts : table ) -> table  -- Create a `wait` dialog node (timed pause).
library.dialog.event( name : string, data : any, opts : table ) -> table  -- Create an `event` dialog node (named hook signal). When executed, fires `seq:on("event", fn)` with (name, data) then advances.
library.dialog.call( fn : function, opts : table ) -> table  -- Create a `call` dialog node (inline Lua callback). When executed, calls `fn()` immediately and advances without pausing.
library.dialog.jump( target : string, opts : table ) -> table  -- Create a `jump` dialog node (label-based control transfer). Execution resumes at the first node in the current script whose `.label` field equals `target`. Unknown targets are silently skipped.
```

### `seq`

```lua
seq:load( nodes : table )  -- Load a new script, replacing any existing one. Call start() afterwards to begin playback.
seq:start(  )  -- Begin playback from the first node.
seq:update( dt : number )  -- Advance per-frame. Call every frame while isActive() is true.
seq:advance(  )  -- Advance past the current line (when state == "waiting" or "typing"). If typing, skips to full reveal first. If waiting, moves to next node.
seq:skip(  )  -- Skip the entire current line instantly (advances to "waiting").
seq:choose( index : number )  -- Select a choice option by 1-based index. Only valid when state == "choice".
seq:setSpeed( cps : number )  -- Set the typewriter reveal speed.
seq:getSpeed(  ) -> number  -- Get the current reveal speed.
seq:getState(  ) -> string  -- Get the current state string.
seq:isActive(  ) -> boolean  -- Returns true while the sequence is in progress (not idle or done).
seq:isWaitingForChoice(  ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when a choice is pending player input.
seq:currentSpeaker(  ) -> string  -- Returns the speaker name of the current "say" node.
seq:currentText(  ) -> string  -- Returns the full text of the current "say" node.
seq:revealedText(  ) -> string  -- Returns only the revealed portion of the current text.
seq:getChoiceText(  ) -> string  -- Returns the prompt text of the current "choice" node.
seq:getChoiceLabels(  ) -> table  -- Returns an array of choice labels for the current "choice" node.
seq:on( event : string, fn : function )  -- Register a callback for a named event. Events: "line" (speaker, text), "choice" (), "finished" (), "done" (), "event" (name, data), "typewrite" (char, full_text).
seq:off( event : string )  -- Unregister all callbacks for a named event.
seq:getEventBus(  ) -> table|nil  -- Return the optional `lurek.patterns` EventBus mirror, or nil when the engine is not present. External systems can subscribe to any of the sequencer's events through the bus without going through `seq:on()`. The canonical event delivery path remains the local handler table, so the bus is purely a parallel observer channel.
```

## `library.doll` {#doll}

> library.doll — Socket-Based Visual Composition Engine
Assembles composite visual objects (characters, vehicles, faces) from
interchangeable Part sprites attached to named Socket positions on a
DollTemplate blueprint. No physics, no collision, no gameplay logic —
purely visual composition with draw ordering.

The library never calls rendering APIs directly; it produces a sorted
draw list via `Doll:getDrawList()` that the caller hands to its renderer
(typically `lurek.render`). The legacy `Doll:draw()` method is retained
as a deprecated no-op for source compatibility.

*65 functions documented, 3 classes*

```lua
library.doll.newPart(  ) -> Part  -- Create a new Part (visual element for attaching to a Doll socket). Parts carry texture, transform, colour, flip, draw-order, and arbitrary key-value attributes. Attach to a Doll socket with `doll:attach()`.
library.doll.newTemplate( name : string ) -> DollTemplate  -- Create a new DollTemplate (socket layout blueprint). A template defines named sockets at fixed positions and rotations. Each socket has an acceptType filter and a drawOrder for z-sorting.
library.doll.newDoll( template : DollTemplate ) -> Doll  -- Create a new Doll (runtime composite instance of a template). A Doll binds a DollTemplate to a world-space transform and holds Part instances attached to template sockets.
library.doll.getAbsoluteScale( entry : table ) -> number, number  -- Get the absolute scale magnitude from a draw-list entry. Strips the sign introduced by flip flags, returning positive values.
```

### `part`

### `doll`

```lua
doll:getPosition(  ) -> number, number  -- Transform Return the world-space position of this doll.
doll:setPosition( x : number, y : number )  -- Set the world-space position of this doll.
doll:getRotation(  ) -> number  -- Return the world-space rotation of this doll in radians.
doll:setRotation( r : number )  -- Set the world-space rotation of this doll in radians.
doll:getScale(  ) -> number, number  -- Return the world-space scale of this doll.
doll:setScale( sx : number, sy : number )  -- Set the world-space scale of this doll.
doll:getTemplate(  ) -> DollTemplate  -- Template Return the DollTemplate this doll was created from.
doll:isVisible(  ) -> boolean  -- Visibility Return true if this doll is currently visible.
doll:setVisible( v : boolean )  -- Set the visibility of this doll.
doll:getBody(  ) -> any body or nil  -- Optional body / user data refs Return the optional physics body reference attached to this doll.
doll:setBody( b : any )  -- Store an optional physics body reference on this doll.
doll:getUserData(  ) -> any user data or nil  -- Return the optional user-data reference on this doll.
doll:setUserData( v : any )  -- Store an optional user-data reference on this doll.
doll:attach( socketName : string, part : Part ) -> boolean  -- Attach a Part to a named socket. Returns false if socket not found, type mismatch, or invalid args.
doll:detach( socketName : string ) -> Part|nil  -- Detach the Part from a socket, returning it.
doll:getPartAt( socketName : string ) -> Part|nil  -- Return the Part attached at `socketName`, or nil.
doll:findSocket( part : Part ) -> string|nil  -- Return the socket name the given Part is attached to, or nil.
doll:detachAll(  )  -- Detach all parts from all sockets.
doll:getAttachedSockets(  ) -> table  -- Return an array of socket names that currently have a part attached.
doll:getEmptySockets(  ) -> table  -- Return an array of socket names that are currently empty.
doll:getDrawList(  ) -> table  -- Compute world-transform draw list sorted by drawOrder. Each entry: {socketName, part, x, y, rotation, scaleX, scaleY, originX, originY, drawOrder}. **Flip behaviour**: Part flip flags produce negative scale values (e.g. scaleX = -2 when flipX is true and doll+part scale = 2). This is intentional — GPU scale-based mirroring. Use `doll.getAbsoluteScale(entry)` if you need the positive magnitude. **Transform order**: Part offset is rotated by socket rotation before being added to the socket position (socket-local space). The combined offset is then scaled by doll scale and rotated by doll rotation. Does NOT filter by part visibility — caller handles that.
doll:draw(  )  -- Deprecated convenience draw shim — retained only as a no-op. The original implementation referenced an undefined global (`lurek`) and a non-existent namespace (`lurek.render`), so the call chain was a silent no-op in every build. Library code must not call rendering APIs directly (per `library.*` conventions), so the correct path now is for the caller to iterate `Doll:getDrawList()` and dispatch the entries to `lurek.render` (or any other renderer) themselves. This method emits a one-time warning on first invocation and then returns immediately. It will be removed in a future major bump.
```

```lua
ResourceManager:isEnabled( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Return whether tick processing is enabled for a named resource.
ResourceManager:setEnabled( name : string, v : boolean )  -- Enable or disable tick processing for a named resource.
ResourceManager:isVisible( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Return the UI visibility hint of a named resource.
ResourceManager:setVisible( name : string, v : boolean )  -- Set the UI visibility hint of a named resource.
ResourceManager:isLocked( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Return whether a named resource is locked against modifications.
ResourceManager:setLocked( name : string, v : boolean )  -- Lock or unlock add/spend for a named resource.
ResourceManager:add( name : string, amount : number ) -> number  -- Add an amount to a named resource. Returns excess that did not fit.
ResourceManager:spend( name : string, amount : number ) -> boolean  -- Spend an amount from a named resource. Returns true on success.
ResourceManager:canAfford( name : string, amount : number ) -> boolean  -- Return true if the named resource has enough available funds.
ResourceManager:getAvailable( name : string ) -> number  -- Return the available amount (value - reserved) of a named resource.
ResourceManager:reserveAmount( name : string, amount : number )  -- Increase the reservation on a named resource.
ResourceManager:unreserveAmount( name : string, amount : number )  -- Decrease the reservation on a named resource (floored at 0).
ResourceManager:getReserved( name : string ) -> number  -- Return the reserved amount of a named resource.
```

## `library.inventory` {#inventory}

> A pure-Lua replacement for the former `lurek.inventory` Rust binding.
Provides ItemStack, Container (fixed/unlimited/expandable), InvItem with tags,
Slot and SlotState, ItemSet, and a full Inventory with equip slots and subsystem flags.

Usage:
local inventory = require("library.inventory")
local bag = inventory.newContainer("bag", "unlimited", 0)
local sword = inventory.newItem("sword")
sword:setWeight(3.5)
sword:setStackLimit(1)
bag:addItem(sword, 1)

*90 functions documented, 6 classes*

```lua
library.inventory.newItem( type_name : string ) -> table  -- Create a lightweight inventory item definition. Each item has a type, weight, size, stack limit, tag set, and a property map.
library.inventory.newItemStack( inv_item : table, quantity : number, max_quantity : number ) -> table  -- Create a counted stack of a single item type.
library.inventory.newSlot( slot_type : string, state : string ) -> table  -- Create a single inventory slot (holds one ItemStack).
library.inventory.newContainer( name : string, mode : string, slot_count : number, max_slots : number ) -> table  -- Create a named container managing a list of slots. For expandable mode, `max_slots` caps how far `expand()` can grow.
library.inventory.newItemSet( name : string ) -> table  -- Create a named item set (bonus condition). All requirements must be satisfied simultaneously for the set to be active.
library.inventory.newInventory(  ) -> table  -- Create a top-level inventory managing containers, equip slots, item sets, and subsystem flags.
```

## `library.item` {#item}

> A pure-Lua replacement for the former `lurek.item` Rust binding.
Provides a type registry, Item objects with tags/stats/meta/owner, capacity-aware
Stacks with positional access, weighted ItemPools with bulk draws, bounded StackHistory,
a StackManager, and functional analysis helpers.

Usage:
local item = require("library.item")
item.defineType("sword", { category="weapon", base_stats={dmg=10}, base_tags={"equippable"} })
local it = item.newItem("sword")
it:addTag("cursed")
print(it:getStat("dmg"))  -- 10


Note (P7 batch C, 0.6.0): the previous file split `M.newStack` and
`M.newStackBuilder` into a base definition followed by a wrapper that
monkey-patched extra methods onto the returned object (former lines
1004-1186 and 1191-1259 respectively). The wrappers were merged into the
original definitions in this revision; functional behaviour is unchanged.

*138 functions documented, 7 classes*

```lua
library.item.clearTypes(  ) -> nil  -- Clear all registered item types (useful between tests). **Note**: Already-created Item objects retain their stats, tags, and category from the definition that existed at creation time.  Clearing the registry does NOT retroactively change existing items.
library.item.defineType( name : string, def : table )  -- Register a new item type definition.
library.item.getType( name : string ) -> table|nil  -- Retrieve a registered type definition, or nil.
library.item.getTypeNames(  ) -> table  -- Return a sorted list of all registered type names.
library.item.newItem( type_name : string ) -> table  -- Create a new item instance. Stats and tags are copied from the type definition; modifications are per-instance. If `type_name` is not registered, a warning is logged and the item receives default `misc` category with empty stats/tags.
library.item.newStack( name : string, capacity : number ) -> table  -- Create a named stack with optional capacity limit. Acts as both a LIFO stack and a positional list.
library.item.newItemPool(  ) -> table  -- Create a weighted loot pool. Supports weighted draw, bulk multi-draw, and unique-draw operations.
library.item.newStackBuilder(  ) -> table  -- Create a stack builder for constructing stacks from a recipe list.
library.item.newStackHistory( max_entries : number ) -> table  -- Create a bounded event history for stack operations.
library.item.newStackManager(  ) -> table  -- Create a named-stack manager.
library.item.newSlot( name : string, capacity : number ) -> table  -- Create a named slot with optional capacity limit. A slot is a bounded named position that holds zero or more items.
library.item.findNOfStat( items : table, stat : string, n : number ) -> table  -- Return 0-based indices of the top N items ranked by a stat (descending).
library.item.groupByStat( items : table, stat_key : string ) -> table  -- Group items by a stat value. Returns map {value -> array of Items}. Items without the stat are grouped under the key false.
library.item.groupByTagPrefix( items : table, prefix : string ) -> table  -- Group items by tag prefix. Returns map {prefix_value -> array of Items}. A tag matches if it starts with `prefix` (e.g. prefix "tier:" matches "tier:1", "tier:2"). Items with no matching tag go under key "".
library.item.findSequences( items : table, stat_key : string ) -> table  -- Find runs (consecutive sequences) of items sharing the same stat value. Returns array of {value, start_idx, length} (1-based start_idx).
library.item.groupByCategory( items : table ) -> table  -- Group items by category. Returns table: category -> {Item, ...}.
library.item.findAtLeastNOfStat( items : table, stat : string, n : number ) -> table  -- Return items where getStat(stat) >= n.
library.item.findTagGroups( items : table ) -> table  -- Group items by shared tag prefix. Returns table: prefix -> {Item, ...}. A "tag group" is a set of items that share at least one tag.
library.item.sortedIndicesByStat( items : table, stat : string, ascending : boolean ) -> table  -- Return 1-based indices sorted by a stat.
library.item.sortedIndicesByCategory( items : table ) -> table  -- Return 1-based indices sorted by category (alphabetical).
```

## `library.lobby` {#lobby}

> Pure-Lua lobby and room management built on `lurek.network`.

Provides room creation, joining, player tracking, ready-check
coordination, host election, and password protection for multiplayer
pre-game lobbies.

Room lifecycle:

1. Server creates a room via `createRoom(name, opts)`.
2. Players join with `joinRoom(name, ...)`.  The first player becomes host.
3. Players toggle ready state with `setReady(ready, ...)`.
4. When `isAllReady()` returns true the host may start the game.
5. Players leave with `leaveRoom(...)`; host is re-elected automatically.
6. An empty room is removed automatically.

Player states: **not-ready** (default on join) → **ready** (via setReady).


Wire format note: messages between peers are encoded with
`lurek.network.pack` / `lurek.network.unpack` (MessagePack — the canonical
ENet payload format). For human-readable persistence (e.g. saved lobby
state), use `lurek.serial.toJson` / `lurek.serial.fromJson`.

## `library.loot` {#loot}

> Lurek2D loot library — designer-friendly weighted RNG, drop DSL, and pity timers.

A pure-Lua loot table system built on `lurek.math.RandomGenerator`. Provides:

* `LootTable` — Walker–Vose alias-method weighted RNG with O(1) sampling.
* `DropSet`   — composable, conditional, multi-roll drop DSL.
* `Pity`      — guaranteed-after-N-misses helper for streak protection.
* `Modifier`  — luck/level/zone weight multipliers applied as a temporary view.

Usage:
local loot = require("library.loot")
local tbl  = loot.fromList({
{id="gold", weight=60},
{id="ring", weight=10, meta={tier="rare"}},
})
local id, meta = tbl:sample()

*32 functions documented, 4 classes*

```lua
library.loot.setDefaultRng( rng : userdata|table )  -- Install a custom default RNG used by `LootTable:sample` when no rng arg is passed.
library.loot.getDefaultRng(  ) -> userdata|table  -- Get the module's current default RNG (resolves on first use).
library.loot.newTable(  ) -> LootTable  -- Create an empty weighted loot table.
library.loot.fromList( entries : table ) -> LootTable  -- Bulk-build a loot table from a list of `{id, weight, meta?}` entries.
library.loot.fromToml( path : string ) -> LootTable  -- Load a loot table from a TOML file via `lurek.filesystem.read` + `lurek.serial.fromToml`. The file must contain an `entries = [...]` array.
library.loot.merge( ... : LootTable ) -> LootTable  -- Combine multiple LootTables into a single new one. Identical IDs sum weights.
library.loot.newDrop(  ) -> DropSet  -- Create a composable drop description.
library.loot.newPity( target_id : string, threshold : integer ) -> Pity  -- Guarantee `target_id` is forced after `threshold` consecutive misses.
library.loot.newModifier(  ) -> Modifier  -- Stack of named weight multipliers applied to a LootTable view.
```

### `LootTable`

```lua
LootTable:setWeight( id : string, w : number ) -> LootTable  -- Adjust an entry's weight (rebuilds alias on next sample).
LootTable:sample( rng : userdata|table? ) -> string, table?  -- O(1) sample one entry from the alias table.
LootTable:sampleN( n : integer, rng : userdata|table?, opts : table? ) -> table  -- Bulk draw N samples.
LootTable:weightOf( id : string ) -> number  -- Current weight of an entry.
LootTable:totalWeight(  ) -> number  -- Total weight of all entries.
LootTable:probability( id : string ) -> number  -- Normalised probability of an entry (0..1).
LootTable:ids(  ) -> table  -- Snapshot of all entry ids.
LootTable:clone(  ) -> LootTable  -- Deep-copy this table.
```

```lua

DropSet:guarantee( id : string, count : integer? ) -> DropSet  -- Always emit `id × count`.

DropSet:explain( context : table ) -> string  -- Human-readable explanation of which clauses would fire.
```

### `Pity`


## `library.narrative` *(partial)* {#narrative}

> Lurek2D narrative library — Ink-flavoured branching narrative interpreter.

Pure-Lua implementation of a usable subset of inkle's Ink scripting language.
Supports knots, diverts, sticky/regular choices, variables, conditional
choices, inline `{var}` and `{fn(arg)}` substitution, tags, visit counters,
and save/resume.

Supported Ink subset:

=== knot_name ===            -- knot header
Plain prose lines.            -- emitted by continue()
-> next_knot                  -- divert
-> END   (or -> DONE)         -- end story
* text                        -- one-shot choice
+ text                        -- sticky choice (re-selectable)
* { condition } text          -- conditional choice
{ variable } / { fn(args) }   -- inline value substitution
# tag                         -- attach tag to last emitted line
VAR name = value              -- declare initial variable
~ name = expr                 -- run-line variable assignment
// line comment               -- ignored

Usage:
local narrative = require("library.narrative")
local story = narrative.compile([[
=== START ===
Hello, {player_name}.
* Greet the king | -> COURT
* Leave silently | -> END
=== COURT ===
The court applauds. # music:fanfare
-> END
]]):start()

*30 functions documented, 1 classes*

```lua
library.narrative.compile( source : string ) -> Story  -- Compile Ink-subset source into a Story program (not yet started).
library.narrative.loadFile( path : string ) -> Story  -- Load and compile a .ink file via `lurek.filesystem.read`.
library.narrative.precompile( source : string ) -> table  -- Produce a serialisable AST blob (cacheable).
library.narrative.fromBytecode( blob : table ) -> Story  -- Restore a precompiled program into a fresh Story.
library.narrative.parseTagList( str )  -- Parse a `# tag1 # tag2` style string into an array.
library.narrative.weightedChoice( choices, rng )  -- Pick a weighted choice from a `{ {text, weight} ... }` list using rng.
library.narrative.formatList( values, conjunction )  -- Format a list of values as natural prose: "a, b, and c".
library.narrative.localiseStory( story, locale )  -- Attach a `{loc:KEY}` localisation pre-processor using `lurek.i18n.t`.
```

```lua
Story:getVar( name )  -- Get a variable.

Story:bindFunction( name, fn )  -- Bind a Lua function callable from inside `{name(arg)}` markers.

Story:offTag( handle )  -- Remove a previously registered tag handler.
Story:onVarChange( name, fn )  -- Register a variable-change handler.

Story:trace( enable )  -- Toggle trace logging via `lurek.log.debug` (requires it to be available).
Story:dumpProfile(  )  -- Profile the current story state (visits + var counts).
Story:save(  )  -- Serialise full state (vars, visits, knot, pc) for `lurek.save`.
Story:resume( state )  -- Restore from a save blob.
```

## `library.netstate` {#netstate}

> Pure-Lua network state synchronization and turn-based game support.

Built on `lurek.network`, provides automatic state replication between peers
with per-key versioning, change callbacks, authority control, delta updates,
and turn-based game management.

**Authority model**: One peer is the *authority* (typically the server).
Only the authority can write state via `set()`. Non-authority peers receive
delta updates and full-state snapshots. Authority is set at construction
and can be toggled with `setAuthority()`.

**Per-key versioning**: Each key maintains its own monotonically increasing
version number. When a delta arrives, only entries whose version exceeds
the locally stored per-key version are applied — preventing stale replays
even under concurrent updates.

**Turn-based protocol**: Optional. When `turnBased = true`, the authority
manages a turn counter and a rotating peer order. `beginTurn()` advances
the turn and broadcasts the change. Clients receive turn events via `onTurn`.

**Wire format**: state deltas and full-state snapshots are encoded with
`lurek.network.pack` / `lurek.network.unpack` (MessagePack — the canonical
ENet payload format). For human-readable persistence (e.g. write a snapshot
to disk for inspection), pair `:getAll()` with `lurek.serial.toJson`.

**Hash helper**: `:hashState()` is a deterministic FNV-1a digest of all
replicated keys/values; useful for desync detection. When a future
`lurek.data.hash` lift lands (P4 candidate), this should delegate.

**Limitation**: `requestFullState()` has no built-in timeout. If the authority
never responds, the client will not receive a snapshot. Callers should
implement their own timer-based retry or use the `onFullStateTimeout`
callback.

*30 functions documented, 1 classes*

```lua
library.netstate.setLogging( enabled : boolean, custom_log : function )  -- Enable or disable debug logging. When enabled, state changes, authority violations, sync events, and turn changes are logged via `lurek.log.debug` (if available) or a custom function. `lurek.log.debug` when available, otherwise logging is silently skipped.
library.netstate.new( host : userdata|nil, opts : table ) -> NetState  -- Create a new network state synchronization manager. The `host` parameter is a `lurek.network` host (server, client, or host). If `opts.authority` is not provided, authority defaults to `host:isServer()` when the host supports it, otherwise `false`. or nil for offline/testing mode (network operations become no-ops). - `channel` (number, default 0): Network channel for messages. - `authority` (boolean): Override authority detection. - `turnBased` (boolean, default false): Enable turn-based protocol. - `maxDirtyKeys` (number|nil): Maximum number of dirty keys tracked per sync cycle. When exceeded, oldest dirty keys are evicted. Nil = unlimited.
```

### `NetState`

```lua
NetState:setAuthority( auth : boolean )  -- Set whether this instance is the authority (can write state).
NetState:isAuthority(  ) -> boolean  -- Check if this instance is the authority.
NetState:onChange( fn : function )  -- Set a global change callback fired for any key change.
NetState:onFullStateTimeout( fn : function )  -- Register a callback invoked if a full-state request times out. The caller is responsible for implementing timer logic and calling this callback from their own timeout handler.
NetState:set( key : string, value : any ) -> boolean, string|nil  -- Set a synced value. Only the authority can set values. Non-authority calls are rejected and return `false, "not authority"`. Keys must be non-empty strings.
NetState:get( key : string ) -> any|nil  -- Get the current value of a synced key.
NetState:getKeyVersion( key : string ) -> number  -- Get the per-key version number.
NetState:getAll(  ) -> table  -- Get all synced state as a flat table.
NetState:onChanged( key : string, fn : function )  -- Register a callback for changes to a specific key.
NetState:clearCallbacks( key : string )  -- Remove all callbacks for a key.
NetState:getVersion(  ) -> number  -- Get the highest version number across all keys.
NetState:getKeyCount(  ) -> number  -- Get the number of synced keys.
NetState:getDirtyCount(  ) -> number  -- Get the number of dirty (unsent) keys.
NetState:hasKey( key : string ) -> boolean  -- Check if a key exists in the state.
NetState:remove( key : string ) -> boolean, string|nil  -- Remove a key from the synced state. Authority only.
NetState:setTurnOrder( order : table )  -- Set the turn order (array of peer IDs). Resets the turn index to 1 and turn counter to 0. Each element must be a number. Invalid entries are silently filtered.
NetState:beginTurn(  ) -> number, number|nil  -- Begin a new turn. Advances to the next player in the turn order. Only the authority should call this. If the turn order is empty, the turn counter advances but `turn_peer` remains nil.
NetState:endTurn(  ) -> number, number|nil  -- End the current turn. Alias for `beginTurn()` — advances to next.
NetState:getCurrentTurn(  ) -> number  -- Get the current turn number.
NetState:getTurnPeer(  ) -> number|nil  -- Get the peer ID whose turn it currently is.
NetState:onTurn( fn : function )  -- Register a callback for turn changes.
NetState:isTurn( peer_id : number ) -> boolean  -- Check if it is a specific peer's turn.
NetState:sync(  )  -- Broadcast all dirty state to connected peers. Call once per frame after all `set()` calls (e.g. at end of `lurek.process(dt)`). Requires a valid host; no-op if host is nil or instance is not authority.
NetState:poll(  ) -> table  -- Process incoming state updates from the network. Call once per frame. Requires a valid host; returns empty table if host is nil.
NetState:_markDirty( key : string )  -- Mark a key as dirty, respecting the maxDirtyKeys limit.
NetState:hashState(  ) -> number  -- Compute a deterministic FNV-1a 32-bit digest of the current synced state. Useful for desync detection between authority and clients (compare digests after a sync round; mismatch indicates state divergence). TODO(P4 lift): when `lurek.data.hash` lands in the engine (P4 lift candidate), this method should delegate to it for the inner string-hashing step.  Until then a small inline FNV-1a implementation keeps the library self-contained and works on both LuaJIT (`bit` library) and Lua 5.4 (native `~`/`&`).
NetState:toJson(  ) -> string|nil  -- Serialise the current state to a JSON string via `lurek.serial.toJson`. Suitable for human-readable persistence (NOT for the wire — use the normal `:sync()` MessagePack path for peer-to-peer traffic). Returns nil if `lurek.serial` is unavailable in this runtime.
NetState:requestFullState(  ) -> boolean  -- Request a full state snapshot from the authority. Useful when a client joins mid-game. **Limitation**: This method has no built-in timeout. If the authority never responds, the client will not receive a snapshot. Callers should implement their own timer-based retry, e.g.: ns:requestFullState() local deadline = lurek.timer.getTime() + 5.0 -- In process loop: if lurek.timer.getTime() > deadline then retry or invoke --   ns:onFullStateTimeout callback
```

This replaces the Lua `setPixel` loop + `detectAdjacency` pass with a single Rust O(w×h) scan. For a 2400×1200 map with 3000 provinces the typical speedup is 100×–300× vs the pure-Lua path (from ~2–8 s down to ~15–30 ms). Each unique non-black RGB pixel in the PNG is automatically assigned a sequential province ID starting at 1. Pure-black pixels (0, 0, 0) become background (ID 0). The returned ProvinceMap has its `pixel_lookup` field replaced by the engine grid (so `getProvinceAt` delegates to it), and all adjacency edges are pre-populated from the single Rust scan. Requires `lurek.image` to be available (Platform Services tier). by `M.loadFromDefinitions` — used to attach names, factions, and other metadata. Pass nil to skip.

## `library.quest` {#quest}

> A pure-Lua replacement for the former `lurek.quest` Rust binding.
No engine dependencies; works in headless test VMs.
Optional: uses `lurek.log.debug()` / `lurek.log.info()` when available for
quest state-change tracing.

**Quest lifecycle states and valid transitions**:

available → active  (via Quest:start)
active    → completed (via Quest:complete)
active    → failed    (via Quest:fail)

All other transitions are rejected and return false.

**Engine integrations** (all optional — inject from your game code):

* Event bus: attach `lurek.patterns.newEventBus()` via `QuestLog:setEventBus`
to receive `quest_started` / `quest_advanced` / `quest_completed` /
`quest_failed` events.
* Serialisation: `M.toJson(log)` / `M.fromJson(str)` round-trip the log
through `lurek.serial.toJson` / `lurek.serial.fromJson`.
* Persistence: register a custom collector with `lurek.save.SaveManager`
that calls `M.toJson(log)` on save and `M.fromJson(str)` on load.
* Time-limited objectives: drive expiry from a `lurek.timer.Scheduler` you
create in your game loop (call `QuestLog:failQuest(id)` from the callback);
this library does not require `lurek.timer` at runtime.

Usage:
local quest = require("library.quest")
local log = quest.newQuestLog()
local q = quest.newQuest("tutorial", "Tutorial Quest")
q.description = "Learn the basics"
local stage = quest.newQuestStage("s1", "First Steps")
local obj = quest.newObjective("talk_npc", "Talk to the guide", 1)
stage:addObjective(obj)
q:addStage(stage)
log:addQuest(q)
log:startQuest("tutorial")
log:advanceObjective("tutorial", "talk_npc", 1)

*56 functions documented, 4 classes*

```lua

library.quest.newQuestStage( id : string, name : string ) -> QuestStage  -- Create a new empty quest stage.
library.quest.newQuest( id : string, title : string, max_journal_entries : number ) -> Quest  -- Create a new quest in the "available" state. Valid status transitions: available → active → completed | failed.
library.quest.newQuestLog(  ) -> QuestLog  -- Create an empty quest log.
library.quest.toJson( log : QuestLog ) -> string  -- Encode a `QuestLog` to a JSON string via `lurek.serial.toJson`.
library.quest.fromJson( str : string, into : QuestLog ) -> QuestLog  -- Decode a JSON-encoded log into a fresh `QuestLog`. The optional `into` argument lets the caller reuse an existing log (its quests are replaced).
```

## `library.rhythm` {#rhythm}

> Lurek2D rhythm library — BPM-locked event sequencer over `lurek.audio`.

Turns audio playhead time into beat-grid scheduling for rhythm games and
music-reactive levels. Two main pieces:

* `Clock`  — beat clock with BPM, swing, ramp, and audio-source binding.
* `M.judge` — judgement window scoring for player input timing.

The clock is independent of `lurek.timer.Scheduler` (which is wall-time
based) — beat math accounts for BPM ramps and audio source seeks.

*26 functions documented, 1 classes*

```lua
library.rhythm.newClock( bpm, opts )  -- Build a free-running BPM clock.
library.rhythm.fromAudio( source, bpm, opts )  -- Build a clock anchored to an `lurek.audio` Source's playhead.
library.rhythm.setJudgementWindows( w : table )  -- Override the default judgement window thresholds (all values in seconds). Keys: `perfect`, `great`, `good`. Omitted keys keep their current values.
library.rhythm.getJudgementWindows(  ) -> table  -- Return the current judgement window thresholds as a table.
library.rhythm.judge( clock : Clock, division : integer, hit_time : number? ) -> string, number  -- Judge a player input against the nearest beat at `division`.
```

### `Clock`

```lua

Clock:start(  ) -> Clock  -- Start (or restart) the clock from beat zero.

Clock:syncToAudio( source : table ) -> Clock  -- Re-anchor the clock to a new audio source's current playhead.

Clock:getBar(  ) -> number  -- Fractional bar number since `:start()` (beat / subdivision).
```

## `library.roguelike` {#roguelike}

> Lurek2D roguelike library — FOV, energy scheduler, and goal maps.

Pure-Lua runtime queries that turn `lurek.tilemap` into a roguelike:

* `Fov`       — symmetric recursive-shadowcasting field of view.
* `Scheduler` — discrete energy/speed turn scheduler (not Δt-based).
* `GoalMap`   — multi-source Dijkstra distance field with flee inversion.

All three subsystems are independent — pick what you need.

*32 functions documented, 3 classes*

```lua
library.roguelike.newFov( opts : table? ) -> Fov  -- Construct a new FOV instance. informational; only "shadowcast" is implemented.
library.roguelike.newScheduler(  ) -> Scheduler  -- Create an action-cost (energy) scheduler. Each actor has a `speed` value; on each `:next()` call the actor with the highest accumulated energy goes. Internal clock advances by the minimum ticks needed to bring at least one actor's energy >= 100.
library.roguelike.newGoalMap( width, height )  -- Construct a goal map of the given grid dimensions.
library.roguelike.bresenham( x0, y0, x1, y1 )  -- Bresenham line of grid points from (x0,y0) to (x1,y1). Falls back to a Lua implementation if `lurek.math.bresenham` is unavailable.

```

## `library.rpc` {#rpc}

> Enables calling functions on remote peers over ENet with automatic
JSON serialisation via `lurek.serial`. Supports request/response,
fire-and-forget, and broadcast patterns.

## RPC Protocol

Messages are serialised via `lurek.serial.toJson` / `lurek.serial.fromJson`.
Three message types flow over the wire:

- **rpc_call**: `{type="rpc_call", id=N, name="fn", args={...}}`
Sender expects an `rpc_response` back with the matching `id`.
- **rpc_response**: `{type="rpc_response", id=N, success=bool, result={...}}`
Returned by the callee after executing the handler for `rpc_call`.
- **rpc_notify**: `{type="rpc_notify", name="fn", args={...}, peer_id=N}`
Fire-and-forget; no response is sent back. `peer_id` is included so
broadcast handlers can identify the originator.

## Error Handling

Set a global error callback via `onError(fn)`. The callback receives a
single string describing the error context (includes the method name when
available). Handler exceptions during `rpc_call` are caught and sent back
as `{success=false, result={error_string}}` to the caller.

## Request ID Limits

The internal request ID counter is a Lua number. In LuaJIT (double-
precision float) integers are exact up to 2^53. Call `resetIdCounter()`
to reset to 1 if your application may exceed this range.

*16 functions documented, 1 classes*

```lua
library.rpc.new( host : userdata, channel : number, timeout : number ) -> RPC  -- Create a new RPC manager attached to a NetworkHost.
```

### `RPC`

```lua
RPC:setLogging( enabled : boolean )  -- Enable or disable debug logging via `lurek.log`. When enabled, RPC calls, responses, and errors are logged at debug level.
RPC:register( name : string, fn : function )  -- Register a function callable from remote peers.
RPC:unregister( name : string )  -- Unregister a previously registered function.
RPC:onError( fn : function )  -- Set a global error handler for RPC processing errors. The callback receives a single string that includes error context (method name, peer ID) when available.
RPC:call( peer_id : number, name : string, callback : function, ... : any ) -> number  -- Call a function on a specific remote peer (request/response pattern). When a matching `rpc_response` arrives in `poll()`, the `callback` is invoked with `(success, result_table)`.
RPC:notify( peer_id : number, name : string, ... : any )  -- Fire-and-forget call: no response expected. Includes `peer_id` in the wire message so broadcast handlers on the receiving side can identify the originator.
RPC:broadcast( name : string, ... : any )  -- Broadcast an RPC call to all connected peers (fire-and-forget). Includes `peer_id = 0` in the wire message (server/broadcast origin).
RPC:resetIdCounter(  )  -- Reset the internal request ID counter back to 1. Useful for long-running servers to avoid exceeding the 2^53 integer precision limit of Lua numbers (LuaJIT doubles). **Warning**: Only call this when no pending calls are in flight.
RPC:getNextId(  ) -> number  -- Get the current request ID counter value.
RPC:getPendingCount(  ) -> number  -- Get the number of pending (unresolved) RPC calls.
RPC:setTimeout( seconds : number )  -- Set the timeout for future pending calls (seconds). 0 = no timeout.
RPC:poll(  ) -> table  -- Process incoming RPC messages. Call once per frame in `lurek.process(dt)`. Dispatches received RPC calls to registered handlers and invokes pending call callbacks when matching responses arrive. Also expires timed-out pending calls.
RPC:_expireTimeouts(  )  -- Internal: expire pending calls that have exceeded their timeout.
RPC:_dispatch( peer_id : number, data : table, responses : table )  -- Internal: dispatch a decoded RPC message.
RPC:getHandlerCount(  ) -> number  -- Get the number of registered RPC handlers.
```

## `library.scheduler` {#scheduler}

> Lurek2D coroutine scheduler — a pure-Lua cooperative task runner.

A pure-Lua coroutine scheduler that integrates with the engine's update loop.
No engine dependencies; works in headless test VMs.

Tasks are coroutine bodies that receive a `yield(seconds)` helper. Calling
`yield(n)` suspends the task for `n` seconds of game time. When the wait
elapses the scheduler resumes the coroutine. Tasks that return (or error)
are automatically removed on the next `update()`.

This is a **coroutine-frame** scheduler: timing is measured in units of `dt`
you pass to `:update(dt)`. For wall-clock one-shots / repeats use the engine
`lurek.timer.Scheduler` userdata (`:after`, `:every`, `:cancel`) instead.

Usage:
local scheduler = require("library.scheduler")
local sched = scheduler.newScheduler()
sched:add(function(yield)
print("start")
yield(1.0)    -- pause for 1 second of game time
print("after 1 second")
end)
-- each frame:
sched:update(dt)

*11 functions documented, 1 classes*

```lua
library.scheduler.newScheduler( opts : table, opts.max_iterations : number ) -> Scheduler  -- Create a new coroutine scheduler. Manages a pool of coroutine tasks; each task can `yield(seconds)` to pause itself. Completed, errored, and removed tasks are cleaned up automatically.
```

### `sched`

```lua
sched:add( fn : function, name : string ) -> number  -- Add a new task function to the scheduler. The task receives a `yield` function as its first argument. Call `yield(seconds)` inside the task to pause for that many seconds.
sched:remove( id : number ) -> boolean  -- Remove a task by id.
sched:pause( id : number )  -- Pause a task by id.  Paused tasks keep their remaining wait time but are not ticked until resumed.
sched:resume( id : number )  -- Resume a paused task by id.
sched:getStatus( id : number ) -> string|nil, string|nil  -- Return the status of a task.
sched:update( dt : number ) -> number  -- Step all active tasks by dt seconds. Tasks whose wait time has elapsed are resumed. A per-call iteration guard prevents infinite loops when a task yields 0 repeatedly.
sched:getCount(  ) -> number  -- Return the number of active (non-done) tasks.
sched:getErrors(  ) -> table  -- Return the list of errors captured since creation (or last `clearErrors()`). Each entry is `{ id = number, msg = string }`.
sched:clearErrors(  )  -- Clear the captured error list.
sched:clear(  )  -- Remove all tasks immediately.
```

### `Buff`

```lua
Buff:isExpired(  ) -> boolean  -- Whether the buff has expired.
```

### `LevelThresholds`

```lua
LevelThresholds:thresholdFor( level : number ) -> number  -- Return the XP required to advance past the given level.
```

### `Sheet`

```lua
Sheet:define( name : string, base : number, opts : table|nil )  -- Define a named attribute.
Sheet:get( name : string ) -> number|nil  -- Get effective value (base * multipliers + additive, clamped).
Sheet:getBase( name )  -- Get raw base value.
Sheet:setBase( name, value )  -- Set base value (clamped to min/max).
Sheet:setMin( name : string, val : number )  -- Set the minimum clamp value for an attribute.
Sheet:setMax( name : string, val : number )  -- Set the maximum clamp value for an attribute.
Sheet:getMin( name : string ) -> number|nil  -- Get the current minimum clamp for an attribute.
Sheet:getMax( name : string ) -> number|nil  -- Get the current maximum clamp for an attribute.
Sheet:setRegen( name : string, val : number )  -- Set the regeneration rate for an attribute.
Sheet:getRegen( name : string ) -> number|nil  -- Get the regeneration rate for an attribute.
Sheet:getStatNames(  )  -- Get all defined attribute names.
Sheet:addBuff( stat : string, add : number, mul : number, duration : number, source : string, stack_mode : string|nil ) -> number|nil  -- Add a buff to the sheet and return its numeric handle. When stack_mode is provided and a duplicate buff exists (same stat + source), the mode controls behavior: None rejects, Duration extends, Intensity increases.
Sheet:removeBuff( handle : number ) -> boolean  -- Remove a buff by its numeric handle.
Sheet:clearBuffs( stat : string|nil )  -- Remove all active buffs, or only those affecting a specific attribute.
Sheet:getBuffs( stat : string|nil ) -> table  -- Return all active (non-expired) buffs as an array of info tables. Each entry has: handle, stat, add, mul, duration, remaining, source.
Sheet:getBuffCount( stat : string|nil ) -> number  -- Count active (non-expired) buffs, optionally limited to one attribute.
Sheet:applyTraitBuffs( trait_name : string ) -> table  -- Apply a registered trait's permanent buffs to this sheet.
Sheet:removeTraitBuffs( trait_name : string ) -> boolean  -- Remove all buffs that were applied by a named trait.
Sheet:hasTrait( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Return true if a named trait is currently active on this sheet.
Sheet:getActiveTraits(  ) -> table  -- Return a sorted list of all currently active trait names.
Sheet:defineSkill( name : string, opts : table|nil )  -- Define a named skill on this sheet.
Sheet:learnSkill( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Advance a skill by one level. Returns false if already at max level or unknown.
Sheet:useSkill( name : string ) -> boolean,  -- Attempt to use a skill: deducts cost and starts cooldown. Returns false plus a reason string on failure.
Sheet:getSkillLevel( name : string ) -> number  -- Get the current level of a named skill (0 = not learned).
Sheet:getCooldownRemaining( name : string ) -> number  -- Get the remaining cooldown in seconds for a named skill.
Sheet:definePerk( name : string, opts : table|nil )  -- Define a named perk on this sheet.
Sheet:acquirePerk( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Acquire a perk if requirements are met. Returns false if already acquired or level too low.
Sheet:hasPerk( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Return true if a named perk has been acquired.
Sheet:setFlag( name : string )  -- Set a boolean flag on this sheet.
Sheet:clearFlag( name : string )  -- Clear (remove) a boolean flag.
Sheet:hasFlag( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Return true if a boolean flag is set.
Sheet:getFlags(  ) -> table  -- Return a sorted list of all set flag names.
Sheet:addXP( amount : number ) -> number  -- Award XP and apply automatic level-ups. Returns the number of levels gained.
Sheet:getXP(  ) -> number  -- Return the current accumulated XP.
Sheet:setXP( v : number )  -- Directly set the accumulated XP (does not trigger level-ups).
Sheet:getLevel(  ) -> number  -- Return the current level.
Sheet:setLevel( v : number )  -- Directly set the character level.
Sheet:setLevelThresholds( t : LevelThresholds )  -- Replace the level threshold configuration.
Sheet:recordUse( name : string )  -- Record a use of a stat for use-based levelling. Applies growth if configured.
Sheet:getUseCount( name : string ) -> number  -- Return the number of recorded uses of an attribute.
Sheet:setActionPoints( max_val : number )  -- Initialise action points with the given maximum (current also set to max).
Sheet:getActionPoints(  ) -> number,  -- Return current and maximum action points.
Sheet:spendActionPoints( amount : number ) -> boolean  -- Spend action points. Returns false if insufficient AP.
Sheet:beginTurn(  )  -- Reset current AP to maximum (call at the start of each turn).
Sheet:recoverActionPoints( amount : number ) -> number  -- Recover AP (partial restore), capped at maximum. Returns new current value.
Sheet:setMorale( max_val : number )  -- Initialise the morale tracker with the given maximum.
Sheet:getMorale(  ) -> number,  -- Return current and maximum morale values.
Sheet:adjustMorale( delta : number )  -- Adjust morale by a delta (positive or negative), clamped to [0, max].
Sheet:setPanicThreshold( val : number )  -- Set the morale value below which the unit enters panic.
Sheet:setBerserkThreshold( val : number )  -- Set the morale value below which the unit goes berserk.
Sheet:checkMorale(  ) -> string|nil  -- Evaluate morale level and update panic/berserk flags.
Sheet:setResistance( dtype : string, val : number )  -- Set resistance to a damage type (clamped to [0, 1]).
Sheet:getResistance( dtype : string ) -> number  -- Return the resistance fraction for a damage type (default 0).
Sheet:applyDamage( stat : string, amount : number, dtype : string|nil ) -> number  -- Apply damage to an attribute, reduced by resistance. Returns actual damage dealt.
Sheet:setEncumbrance( cur : number, max_val : number )  -- Set current encumbrance and its maximum capacity.
Sheet:getEncumbrance(  ) -> number,  -- Return current and maximum encumbrance values.
Sheet:isEncumbered(  ) -> boolean  -- Return true if current weight exceeds the encumbrance limit.
Sheet:setInitiative( val : number )  -- Set the sheet's base initiative value.
Sheet:getInitiative(  ) -> number  -- Return the current initiative value.
Sheet:update( dt : number )  -- Advance time: tick buff durations, skill cooldowns, apply regen.
Sheet:snapshot(  ) -> table  -- Capture a snapshot of the sheet's core state (attributes, XP, level, flags, resistances, AP, morale).
Sheet:restore( snap : table )  -- Restore sheet state from a snapshot previously created by Sheet:snapshot.
```
